name: Vía Verde de la Jara Loop
name_original: Puerto de San Vicente a Antigua estación Santa Quiteria — circular por La Estrella desde Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé
description: This moderate 37.0-mile mountain biking loop on the Vía Verde de la Jara offers a unique ride through tunnels and viaducts.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Aldeanueva De San Bartolomé offers diverse landscapes within the Comarca de la Jara, characterized by extensive Mediterranean forests, traditional *dehesas*, and olive groves. The region features the granite Cerro del Castrejón, rising to 680 meters, providing panoramic views. Various waterways, including the Huso River, shape verdant valleys, while the Vía Verde de la Jara, a former railway line, provides a mostly flat greenway for cyclists. Best mountain bike trails around Aldeanueva De San Bartolomé The most popular mountain bike trail is Vía Verde de la Jara Loop, a 36.9 miles (59.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 6 minutes to complete. This route follows a historic greenway, offering an easy, mostly flat terrain suitable for various skill levels. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Campillo de la Jara – circular desde Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé por el Cerro Castrejón, a moderate 27.3 miles (43.9 km) path. This circular route starts from Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé and explores the area around the prominent Cerro Castrejón. Local mountain bikers also love the Puerto de Arrebatacapas loop from Mohedas de la Jara, a 30.4 miles (48.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Aldeanueva De San Bartolomé is defined by Mediterranean forests, *dehesas*, and the historic Vía Verde de la Jara. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aldeanueva De San Bartolomé's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, from extensive Mediterranean forests and traditional *dehesas* to olive groves. You'll encounter varied terrain, including the granite Cerro del Castrejón and verdant valleys shaped by waterways like the Huso River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350476821/via-verde-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, follows a mostly flat greenway, while other routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24531171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arrebatacapas loop from Mohedas de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé?
A: Yes, there are options for various skill levels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350476821/via-verde-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for beginners and families, as it follows a former railway line with easy, mostly flat terrain and minimal inclines. There are 2 easy-rated tours in the area.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in this area?
A: The mountain bike trails around Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé cater to a range of abilities. You'll find 2 easy routes, 9 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for casual riders to experienced mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A notable one is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025236912/el-campillo-de-la-jara-circular-desde-aldeanueva-de-san-bartolome-por-el-cerro-castrejon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Campillo de la Jara – circular desde Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé por el Cerro Castrejón&lt;/a&gt;, which starts directly from the town and explores the area around the prominent Cerro Castrejón.

Q: What points of interest can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Along the trails, you can explore various natural and historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2552694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; itself is a major attraction, featuring numerous tunnels and viaducts. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3853975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huso River Valley&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4253507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arrebatachacas&lt;/a&gt;, and remnants of ancient settlements on Cerro del Castrejón.

Q: Is Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé a good base for exploring the Vía Verde de la Jara?
A: While the Vía Verde de la Jara doesn't pass directly through Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé, it is a key accessible attraction in the broader La Jara region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350476821/via-verde-de-la-jara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that allows you to experience this spectacular greenway.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, while winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round riding, though some routes might be more challenging in wet conditions.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders looking for longer distances and more elevation, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24531192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Quiteria Station – San Vicente Pass loop from Mohedas de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;, at over 50 km, offer a significant challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24531171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arrebatacapas loop from Mohedas de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; also provides varied terrain and substantial elevation gain.

Q: What kind of natural features will I encounter on the trails?
A: You'll ride through diverse natural settings, including extensive oak and pine forests, traditional *dehesas* (wooded pastures), and olive groves. The landscape is also shaped by rivers like the Huso, creating verdant valleys, and you'll find the granite Cerro del Castrejón offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Routes that ascend or circle the Cerro del Castrejón, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025236912/el-campillo-de-la-jara-circular-desde-aldeanueva-de-san-bartolome-por-el-cerro-castrejon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Campillo de la Jara – circular desde Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé por el Cerro Castrejón&lt;/a&gt;, will provide excellent panoramic views of the surrounding Comarca de la Jara due to its elevation of 680 meters.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat near the mountain bike trails?
A: Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé and the surrounding towns in the Comarca de la Jara offer various services, including accommodation and places to eat. While specific cyclist-focused services aren't detailed in the route data, the region is accustomed to outdoor enthusiasts, so you can find local establishments to refuel and rest.
tips: In this town of interesting popular rural architecture is the Church of Saint Bartholomew, known in the surrounding area as the "Sistine Chapel of Jara", the region in which it is located, due to the frescoes by Wladimir Strashko, which represent scenes from life. and martyrdom of Saint Bartholomew.

The Old Station of Santa Quiteria is a point of interest on the Vía Verde de la Jara, located in Sevilleja de la Jara, Toledo. This station was a strategic stopping point at the Puerto de San Vicente, where the old Roman road that connected Toledo (Toletum) with Mérida (Augusta Emerita) passed.

Today, the station is abandoned, but it remains a fascinating place for history and nature lovers. It is a popular spot for hiking and cycling routes, offering a window into the past and an opportunity to enjoy the natural surroundings.

Another of the various stations found along the route of the Vía Verde de la Jara. This particular building used to function as a hotel and restaurant, although unfortunately it is currently in ruins.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía Verde de la Jara Loop?
faqs.answer: While the tour starts near Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé, you can find parking options in various towns along the Vía Verde, such as Calera y Chozas, Aldeanueva de Barbarroya, or Campillo-Señor. These towns offer convenient access points to the trail.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde de la Jara Loop suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered easy and ideal for all skill levels, including beginners and families. Its mostly flat profile, following an old railway line, makes it a relaxed ride. Komoot classifies it as 'Easy' (STS S0 - S1).

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Vía Verde de la Jara Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of compacted earth and gravel paths, with some sections potentially asphalted. As it follows a former railway line, the route is largely flat with minimal inclines, making it suitable for mountain bikes and gravel bikes.

faqs.question: What are the notable features or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is known for its beautiful natural surroundings, including granite and slate formations, and in early spring, endless fields of flowers. A distinctive feature is the presence of 18 tunnels and 6 viaducts, with one tunnel stretching for a kilometer. You'll also pass by the Church of San Bartolomé, Santa Quiteria Station, and the Former Campillo-Sevilleja Station.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses a virgin landscape between the Montes de Toledo and the Vega del Tajo, offering opportunities to observe local wildlife. You might spot various bird species and deer in this least populated area of Spain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride the Vía Verde de la Jara Loop?
faqs.answer: Early spring is particularly beautiful, with endless fields of flowers. However, the trail is popular year-round, especially on weekends. Consider the weather, as some sections may be in poor condition after heavy rains.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this ride?
faqs.answer: It's crucial to bring plenty of water, as there are no fountains along the route. A front light is essential for navigating the unlit tunnels. Be aware that some sections may be in poor condition, so a well-maintained bike is recommended.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on the Vía Verde de la Jara Loop?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, Vías Verdes in Spain are generally dog-friendly. It's always best practice to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to maintain the trail's natural beauty.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Vía Verde de la Jara Loop. It is a public recreational path.

faqs.question: Does the Vía Verde de la Jara Loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino de la Jara - Etapa 3, Vía Verde de la Jara, Camino Natural de Las Villuercas, and Camino de la Jara - Etapa 2, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde de la Jara Loop a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this specific tour, 'Vía Verde de la Jara Loop', is designed as a circular route, allowing you to start and end in the same general area, such as Aldeanueva de San Bartolomé.
